heroHeadline: US Seizes Third Iranian Oil Tanker; Talks Stalled as Trump Orders Navy to Shoot Mine-Laying Boats
heroSubtitle: On Day 55, the US Navy seized the M/T Majestic X in the Indian Ocean — the third vessel detained under the Iran blockade in four days — as the dual-blockade standoff intensified. Trump issued a social media order directing the Navy to 'shoot and kill' any boat laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, as satellite imagery showed 33 IRGC fast-attack craft massing near the Kargan coast. Peace negotiations remain fully stalled: Iran refuses to attend a second Islamabad round, the Senate rejected Democrats' fifth war powers challenge (51-46), and crews of the seized MSC Francesca and Epaminondas remain aboard vessels heading to Bandar Abbas. Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil and four others; Lebanon and Israel held rare direct talks in Washington seeking to extend the Hezbollah ceasefire. The USS George H.W. Bush carrier group — with an amphibious assault ship carrying 2,500 Marines — is en route to the theater.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-23. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, ABC News, NBC News, PBS NewsHour, Xinhua, Euronews, UN News, Axios, CFR, Time, Washington Post, Washington Times, Military.com, Stars and Stripes, RTÉ News, Al-Monitor, Lloyd's List.
